name: Operations/ms-reindex
description: $reindex operation conformance tests ported from Microsoft.Health.Fhir.Shared.Tests.E2E.Rest.Reindex.ReindexTests. The full E2E suite exercises long-running async reindex jobs (hundreds of SearchParameter creations, waiting up to 300s for job completion) which this engine cannot represent because it has no job-polling loop primitive and request bodies must come from static fixtures. Instead this suite covers what is reliably testable without needing a job to fully complete: kickoff (POST $reindex, expected 201 Created with a Content-Location job-status header per ReindexController.CreateReindexJob), a single status poll via that header, GET $reindex (list active jobs), and per-resource $reindex (POST {type}/{id}/$reindex, which is synchronous and returns 200 immediately). The $reindex operation is commonly disabled on non-reference or hardened deployments (ReindexController.CheckIfReindexIsEnabledAndRespond throws a 400 RequestNotValidException when disabled) and may not exist at all on non-Microsoft-FHIR-Server targets, so every status/shape assertion here is warningOnly.

variable.name: reindexJobLocation
variable.description: The job-status URL from the Content-Location header of the kickoff response (ReindexController sets this via SetContentLocationHeader). Falls back to GET $reindex (list jobs) if the header was absent, e.g. because reindex is disabled.

variable.name: reindexTargetPatientId
variable.description: Server-assigned id of the Patient created for the per-resource $reindex test

operation.description: Create a synthetic Patient to use as the target of the per-resource $reindex test

test.name: $reindex kickoff returns 201 Created with a Content-Location job-status header
test.description: POST $reindex with an empty Parameters (no tuning options) must return 201 Created (ReindexController.CreateReindexJob) with a Content-Location header pointing to the job status resource. warningOnly because $reindex is frequently disabled on non-reference deployments (returns 400 instead) or absent entirely on non-Microsoft-FHIR-Server targets (404).

assert.description: Kickoff should return 201 Created (warningOnly: $reindex may be disabled or unimplemented on this target)
Response body should be a Parameters resource describing the new job (warningOnly)
Response should include a Content-Location header with the job status URL (warningOnly)

test.name: $reindex kickoff accepts maximumNumberOfResourcesPerQuery / maximumNumberOfResourcesPerWrite tuning parameters
test.description: POST $reindex with maximumNumberOfResourcesPerQuery=2 and maximumNumberOfResourcesPerWrite=1 (as used by ReindexTests.GivenReindexJobWithConcurrentUpdates_ThenReportedCountsAreLessThanOriginal) must also return 201 Created. warningOnly for the same environment-dependent reasons as the basic kickoff test.

assert.description: Kickoff with tuning parameters should return 201 Created (warningOnly)

test.name: GET $reindex lists any active reindex jobs
test.description: GET $reindex (no resource prefix) must return 200 with a Parameters resource; this is the mechanism ReindexTests itself uses (CancelAnyRunningReindexJobsAsync) to discover running jobs before each test. warningOnly since $reindex may be disabled/unimplemented.

assert.description: Response body should be a Parameters resource (warningOnly)

test.name: $reindex with an invalid Prefer header value is rejected with 400 Bad Request
test.description: ValidateReindexRequestFilterAttribute runs before the enabled/disabled check and rejects any Prefer header whose value is not 'respond-sync' with a 400 RequestNotValidException, regardless of whether $reindex itself is enabled. This is the most reliable validation-failure scenario for this operation. warningOnly because a non-Microsoft-FHIR-Server target may not implement this specific header check (or the $reindex route at all).

operation.description: POST $reindex with an unsupported Prefer header value

assert.description: Server should reject the unsupported Prefer header with 400 Bad Request (warningOnly)

test.name: $reindex with a non-numeric maximumNumberOfResourcesPerQuery is rejected with 400 Bad Request
test.description: ReindexController.ReadNumericParameter throws a 400 RequestNotValidException when maximumNumberOfResourcesPerQuery cannot be parsed as an integer. warningOnly since $reindex may be disabled/unimplemented, and some servers may coerce or ignore invalid tuning parameters instead of rejecting them.

assert.description: Server should reject the non-numeric parameter with 400 Bad Request (warningOnly)

test.name: Reindex job status can be polled via the Content-Location URL
test.description: GET the URL captured from the kickoff response's Content-Location header (ReindexController.GetReindexJob) must return 200 with a Parameters resource containing a 'status' parameter. warningOnly throughout: depends on the kickoff test above having actually returned a Content-Location header.

operation.description: GET the reindex job status URL

assert.description: Response should be a Parameters resource with a 'status' parameter (warningOnly)

test.name: Per-resource $reindex synchronously reindexes a single resource
test.description: POST {type}/{id}/$reindex (ReindexController.ReindexSingleResource) is synchronous, unlike the system-level job kickoff, and returns 200 with a Parameters result immediately. warningOnly since $reindex may be disabled/unimplemented.

assert.description: Response body should be a Parameters resource (warningOnly)

operation.description: Teardown: delete the Patient created for the per-resource $reindex test
